violations.description: Queue-card assertions are conditional, so tests may pass even when shared primitives fail to render.
violations.suggestion: Seed deterministic fixture data and assert queue-card/pill primitives unconditionally.

violations.description: Thread-detail validation path has multiple fallback branches that weaken deterministic coverage.
violations.suggestion: Stabilize fixture setup so thread-header path is always exercised in this scenario.

violations.description: Per-item safety assertions can be skipped when operatorCopy is empty.
violations.suggestion: Assert minimum non-empty primary copy contract before suppression checks.

violations.description: Aria-label validation is split across fallback branches, reducing consistency of assertion scope.
violations.suggestion: Standardize primary-action test id contract and assert one deterministic path.

recommendations: Eliminate fallback-only branches in core P0 scenarios
Require deterministic fixture data so primitives always render in ATDD runs
Avoid skipping per-item copy assertions when projection data is empty
summary: Determinism is generally solid (no hard waits/randomness), but conditional fallback logic weakens assertion strictness.
